saints.name: Translation of the relics of Saint Nikephoros, Patriarch of Constantinople

troparion.text: Your fragrant relics have been revealed as godly treasure / filled with life for the Church. / On their august translation / we receive the gifts of the Spirit. / Righteous Father Nikephorus, entreat Christ our God to grant us His great mercy.

kontakion.text: You laid down your life for your flock / Inspiring it with true piety. / Therefore it joyfully receives your holy relics and cries to you: / You are our boast and refuge, / O Father Nikephorus, blessed light of confession!

troparion.text: Your holy martyr Sabinus, O Lord, / through his suffering has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through his intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr Sabinus;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.

saints.name: Martyrs Africanus, Publius, and Terence, of Carthage

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

By sharing in the ways of the Apostles, / you became a successor to their throne. / Through the practice of virtue, you found the way to divine contemplation, O inspired one of God; / by teaching the word of truth without error, you defended the Faith, / even to the shedding of your blood. / Hieromartyr Publius, entreat Christ God to save our souls.

Your holy martyr Alexander, O Lord, / through his suffering has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through his intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr Alexander;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.

troparion.text: Your lamb Christina calls out to You, O Jesus, in a loud voice:  /  “I love You, my Bridegroom, and in seeking You I endure suffering.  /  In Baptism I was crucified so that I might reign in You,  /  and I died so that I might live with You.  /  Accept me as a pure sacrifice,  /  for I have offered myself in love.”  /  Through her prayers, save our souls, since You are merciful.

By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Aninas, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!
